concepts.title: Physical State of Alcohols
concepts.description: Alcohols are polar in nature and have high boiling points due to hydrogen bonding. The physical state of alcohols ranges from gas (methanol) to solid (higher alcohols like propan-1-ol and butan-1-ol).

concepts.title: Preparation of Phenol
concepts.description: Phenol is prepared by the Kolbe's electrolysis of sodium phenoxide. It is an important method for the production of phenol on a large scale.

concepts.title: Esterification Reaction
concepts.description: Esterification is the reaction between an alcohol and a carboxylic acid to form an ester and water. This reaction is generally catalyzed by an acid such as H2SO4 or HCl.

concepts.title: Reaction of Alcohols with HCl
concepts.description: To remember the reaction of alcohols with HCl, use the mnemonic 'CH3CH2OH + HCl -> CH3CH2Cl + H2O'. This tells us that primary alcohols react with HCl to form primary alkyl halides.

concepts.title: Substitution of Hydroxyl Group
concepts.description: Students often confuse the substitution of the hydroxyl group in alcohols with the substitution of other functional groups. However, the hydroxyl group is substituted only under special conditions such as with POCl3 or PCl5.

concepts.title: Boiling Point of Alcohols
concepts.description: The boiling point of alcohols increases with the increase in molecular weight due to the increase in the number of hydrogen bonding, however it decreases as the size of the alkyl group increases due to the decrease in the surface tension of the liquid.

concepts.title: Reactions of Aldehydes and Ketones
concepts.description: Aldehydes and ketones undergo nucleophilic addition reactions, such as addition of Grignard reagents and addition of hydrogen cyanide, to form addition products.

concepts.title: Acidic Nature of Phenol
concepts.description: Phenol is a weak acid due to the resonance stabilization of the phenoxide ion. It has a pKa value of around 10, which is lower than that of water.
